saved_test.name: Argumentation and Academic Tone: V3 All Levels
saved_test.description: Page-local V3 package for Argumentation and Academic Tone with A1-C2 academic-English coverage.

questions.question: Choose the best academic-English option for academic argumentation: {a1}

a1.answer: The paragraph develops a clear academic argument
options: The paragraph develops a clear academic argument
The paragraph just says the writer is right
The paragraph attacks the other side angrily
The paragraph proves everything without support

questions.question: Choose the best academic-English option for claim evidence reasoning: {a1}

a1.answer: The claim is supported by evidence and reasoning
options: The claim is supported by evidence and reasoning
The claim is supported by feelings only
The claim needs no evidence at all
The claim is true because it sounds strong

questions.question: Choose the best academic-English option for qualifying claims: {a1}

a1.answer: The conclusion should be qualified because the data are limited
options: The conclusion should be qualified because the data are limited
The conclusion should be absolute because the data are limited
The conclusion proves everything despite limited data
The conclusion should ignore the limits

questions.question: Choose the best academic-English option for counterargument and concession: {a1}

a1.answer: Although the objection is valid the claim remains plausible
options: Although the objection is valid the claim remains plausible
Therefore the objection is valid the claim remains plausible
Because the objection is valid however the claim remains plausible
The objection is stupid so the claim wins

questions.question: Choose the best academic-English option for contrastive argument links: {a1}

a1.answer: The sample was small however the pattern was consistent
options: The sample was small however the pattern was consistent
The sample was small therefore the pattern was consistent
The sample was small because the pattern was consistent
The sample was small and proves all patterns

questions.question: Choose the best academic-English option for result and conclusion links: {a1}

a1.answer: The evidence is consistent therefore the conclusion is plausible
options: The evidence is consistent therefore the conclusion is plausible
The evidence is consistent however the conclusion follows
The evidence is consistent although the conclusion follows
The evidence is consistent but unrelated

questions.question: Choose the best academic-English option for avoiding unsupported claims: {a1}

a1.answer: The claim requires further evidence
options: The claim requires further evidence
The claim is true without evidence
The claim is obviously final
The claim cannot be questioned
